hitchens_question: When did we become so comfortable with the fact that the most significant technological advances in human civilization—artificial intelligence, robotics, satellite imagery, autonomous systems—are being perfected not in laboratories dedicated to curing disease or exploring space, but in the service of making it easier to kill people we've never met in countries we can't locate on a map? The same drones that we celebrate for "turning the tide" in Ukraine today will be sold to the highest bidder tomorrow, and yet we discuss this arms race with the breezy detachment of sports commentators, as if the proliferation of autonomous killing machines were merely another market opportunity rather than the wholesale privatization of death itself.
